---
title: "Should You Buy a Home — and Where?"
author: "Walter Hall"
subtitle: "A data-driven narrative about renting, buying, and real estate opportunity"
format: html
---

## The Housing Decision Isn’t Simple Anymore

For years, buying a home was treated as the obvious path to financial stability. But rising home prices, higher mortgage rates, and uneven local markets have changed the math.

This project explores two connected questions:

1. When does it make more sense to rent instead of buy?
2. In which housing markets does buying still offer strong long-term potential?

## Why This Matters

Housing is not just a monthly payment. It affects wealth-building, mobility, and long-term financial security.

This project uses data to show that the answer is not just **whether** you should buy a home, but also **where**.

## What This Project Will Show

This narrative will examine:

- trends in home prices, rents, and mortgage rates
- the changing cost of ownership over time
- why location changes the rent-versus-buy decision
- which markets may offer stronger investment opportunities

## Roadmap

The full story will eventually include:

- a housing market overview
- a rent vs. buy cost comparison
- an interactive decision tool
- market comparisons across cities
- a final summary infographic
